good.short: Even, calm tone — keep your barrier happy.
long: Stick with a gentle, fragrance-free cleanser.
Daily broad-spectrum SPF 30+ prevents reactive flushing.
Avoid hot water and harsh scrubs.

warn.short: Mild flush. Cool it down with calming actives.
long: Look for centella asiatica (cica) or panthenol serums.
Add azelaic acid 10% a few nights a week for redness control.
Identify triggers — spicy food, alcohol, very hot showers.

bad.short: High redness — focus on barrier repair, not exfoliation.
long: Pause retinoids, AHAs/BHAs until skin calms.
Layer a ceramide + cholesterol + fatty-acid moisturiser.
If persistent, consider a derm visit for rosacea screening.

good.short: Even tone — keep protecting it from UV.
long: Reapply SPF every 2–3 hours of outdoor exposure.
A topical vitamin C in the morning keeps tone bright.
Wide-brim hats are underrated.
Niacinamide 5% morning and night fades mild spots over weeks.
Tranexamic acid serum or azelaic acid help post-inflammatory marks.
Religious SPF is non-negotiable — pigment darkens fast in the sun.
Discuss prescription tretinoin or hydroquinone with a dermatologist.
Chemical peels (mandelic, glycolic) over weeks help fade depth.
Continue daily SPF and avoid picking spots — friction worsens it.
Antioxidants (vitamin C, E) by day, retinol at night.
Adequate sleep and hydration show on the face.
Don't forget the neck and back of the hands.

warn.short: Early fine lines. Time to layer in retinoids.
long: Start a low-strength retinol 2–3 nights/week.
Peptide creams support collagen synthesis.
Hyaluronic acid plumps the surface temporarily — pair with occlusion.

bad.short: Deeper lines — combine actives + professional options.
long: Prescription tretinoin is the gold standard for fine lines.
In-office: microneedling, radiofrequency, or laser resurfacing.
Daily SPF cuts further photoaging dramatically.

good.short: Small, smooth pores. Maintain with regular cleansing.
long: Stick with a non-comedogenic moisturiser.
Niacinamide keeps pores looking refined.
Don't squeeze — it stretches the opening.

warn.short: Visible pores in oilier zones. BHA helps.
long: Salicylic acid (BHA) 2% twice a week clears congestion.
Use a clay mask weekly on the T-zone.
Niacinamide + zinc serum balances oil production.
Topical retinoids reduce apparent pore size over months.
Consider in-office RF microneedling for tightening.
Daily SPF prevents collagen loss around pores.

good.short: Bright under-eye — keep it that way.
long: Use a dedicated eye cream with peptides.
Sleep on your back to reduce morning puffiness.
SPF around the eye area matters too.
Caffeine eye creams reduce vascular darkness.
Vitamin K and niacinamide brighten over weeks.
Cold compress in the morning shrinks puffiness fast.
Check iron levels and sleep quality with a doctor.
Hyperpigmented circles respond to azelaic acid or kojic acid.
Tear-trough fillers can mask anatomical shadowing.

good.short: Clear skin — keep the routine simple.
long: Don't overload with actives — less is more here.
Clean pillowcases weekly cut bacterial load.
Change masks/headbands often during workouts.
Spot-treat with benzoyl peroxide 2.5% overnight.
Salicylic acid 2% as a leave-on every other day.
Avoid heavy occlusive creams on oily zones.
Book a dermatologist — topical/oral retinoids may be warranted.
Adapalene 0.1% over-the-counter is a solid starting point.
Track triggers (dairy, high-glycemic food) for 6 weeks.
